label: near_miss

case_target.description: 当前 case 的主控制对象是 ProxyService._is_safe_url()，它负责执行协议、域名与原始 IP 字面量校验。

should_not_report.reason: ProxyService._is_safe_url() validates the URL against the modeled SSRF policy before get_url() is called. The guard enforces https-only scheme, domain allowlist, and raw-IP-literal range blocking. utils/http_utils.py:get_url() only ever receives pre-validated URLs from the service layer. allow_redirects=False in get_url() prevents redirect-based bypass.
should_not_report.false_positive_risk: A system that inspects utils/http_utils.py in isolation will see requests.get(url) with an externally supplied argument and flag it as an unguarded SSRF sink. A system that traces the call from the route layer but fails to enter _is_safe_url() inside fetch_remote() will reach get_url() via the service and still incorrectly flag requests.get() at utils/http_utils.py line 6.

controls.description: Scheme allowlist: only 'https' is permitted, blocking http://, file://, gopher://, dict:// and other dangerous schemes.
Domain allowlist: only api.example.com and cdn.example.com are permitted, blocking arbitrary external or internal hostnames.
controls.code_snippet: if hostname is None or hostname not in _ALLOWED_DOMAINS:

controls.description: IP-range blocklist: applies only to raw IP literals in the hostname field. If the hostname is a bare IP address (e.g. 127.0.0.1), private/loopback/link-local ranges are rejected, blocking 127.x, 10.x, 192.168.x, 169.254.x etc. Hostnames such as api.example.com are not DNS-resolved for IP checking — the domain allowlist is the control for those.
controls.code_snippet: if addr.is_private or addr.is_loopback or addr.is_link_local:

controls.description: allow_redirects=False prevents the HTTP client from following 3xx redirects that could redirect to an internal host after passing the initial URL validation in the service layer.

notes: Difficulty is 'hard': guard and sink are in different layers/files. get_url() is an intentionally low-level HTTP helper that trusts callers to validate URLs — the near-miss tests whether the system correctly tracks that only pre-validated URLs reach it. allow_redirects=False is in the utils layer as a defense-in-depth measure complementing the service-layer guard.
